text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 27 October 2019

Dr A. Patel
Riverside Health Centre

12 Market Street
Brixton
London SW9 8HQ
Dear Dr A. Patel
Thomas Green, DOB: 14-03-1986, Hospital No: G739215 NHS No. 8473926150
29 Hawthorn Close, Lambeth, London SE11 5DR 

Diagnoses/summary: Recurrent unprovoked seizures, aetiology under investigation; prior routine and sleep-deprived EEGs non-diagnostic

MRI brain (2017) structurally normal; no clear epileptogenic focus identified

Medication history: Levetiracetam previously stopped due to mood symptoms; sodium valproate avoided given family planning discussions in the past; currently on lamotrigine monotherapy with gradual titration through 2019

Not tried to date: topiramate, zonisamide, lacosamide, perampanel, clobazam (regular), brivaracetam

Comorbidities: Generalised anxiety features; receiving ongoing psychological support via IAPT

I reviewed Thomas Green in clinic today. He attended alone. He reports that with ongoing psychological support, coping has improved and he feels more confident managing early warnings and post-event recovery. He has implemented sleep regularity and reduced alcohol, and his partner reports fewer prolonged postictal phases. He has had no injuries, no emergency department attendances, and no status episodes. He is driving abstinent and adherent to DVLA guidance.

Seizure diary review: Focal seizure: 2019: May x0, Jun x0, Jul x1, Aug x0, Sep x1, Oct x2. He notes brief déjà vu and rising anxiety on two October days with preserved awareness and no loss of posture; each lasted under a minute with rapid recovery. July and September events were similar, without secondary generalisation.

Current treatment:
- Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily (titrated from July 2019)
- Folic acid 5 mg daily
- As-required oral ondansetron for nausea (rarely used)

Adverse effects: Mild intermittent dizziness during up-titration, now settled. No rash. No cognitive concerns.

Investigations/monitoring: Routine bloods in September 2019 normal (FBC, U&Es, LFTs). Vitamin D borderline low; supplementation discussed. No new imaging indicated at present.

Plan:
- Continue lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily; maintain adherence and seizure diary.
- Reinforce sleep hygiene, stress management, and avoidance of known precipitants; continue psychological therapy which appears to be contributing to improved coping.
- Safety: continue to avoid driving; first aid and bathing precautions reiterated.
- If two or more further events occur within the next eight weeks, we will consider dose optimisation of lamotrigine or introduction of an add-on therapy after discussion of options and side-effect profiles.
- Arrange telephone review with the epilepsy nurse specialist in 6–8 weeks; routine clinic review in 4 months, earlier if needed.

Overall, his condition is improving, with better coping strategies, fewer prolonged postictal symptoms, and stable daily functioning.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter provides a seizure diary with monthly counts for May–October 2019: May 0, Jun 0, Jul 1, Aug 0, Sep 1, Oct 2. Summing seizures: 1 (Jul) + 1 (Sep) + 2 (Oct) = 4 seizures over a 6-month period (May through October). Events occurred in October, so not seizure free. Thus the normalized frequency is 4 per 6 month.
